_meta.summary: English card set expanded to 20 entries for MVP-2 PlayCard playtest (issue #41). 6 entries are OCR-decoded from documentation/English_Base_EN.txt (Watchman, Billman, Welsh Infantry, Longbowman, Esquire, English Knight — original issue #10 subset). The remaining 14 entries are PLAYTEST STUBS invented by Sabine to exercise the broader Card discriminated union (mix of unit / action / tactic / upgrade / technology) and give MVP-2 PlayCard a deeper deck. The PlayCard MVP-2 path treats every card kind generically (discard + draw 1), so stub variety is purely for playtest flavor and to surface schema coverage gaps. Stat bar order confirmed as melee / health / ranged from inter-card comparison (Welsh Infantry-Archer 3/3/2 vs Longbowman ARCHER 0/2/3 vs Esquire CAVALRY 2/5/0). Movement value sits inline on the class band ('2 INFANTRY' = 2 movement, INFANTRY class). Image placeholders deferred to follow-up real-art ingest (see _meta.images_status). All stub stats and costs MUST be refined in the #17 OCR pass when the real card backs are available.

stub_status.note: playtest_stubs entries are STUBS invented for MVP-2 playtest variety (issue #41). They MUST be refined or replaced against the real English card backs during the #17 OCR pass. Each stub carries a per-card `_needsConfirmation` block describing the stub status.

_meta.images_status: DEFERRED — apps/web/public/cards/english/ holds a README explaining placeholders. Real card art will land in a follow-up issue (likely tied to #17 OCR pass which has access to the binary PDF).

stats_confidence.movement: HIGH — all 6 units show '2 <CLASS>' on the class band; consistent across rulebook examples.
stats_confidence.melee: HIGH — stat-bar top number aligns with thematic class (Longbowman 0, Knight 4).
stats_confidence.health: HIGH — stat-bar middle number aligns with thematic durability (Esquire 5, Knight 7, Longbowman 2).
stats_confidence.ranged: HIGH — stat-bar bottom number is 0 for pure infantry/cavalry, >0 only for ARCHER class.
stats_confidence.cost: LOW — OCR extracted cost icons as bare numbers without resource-type identification (multi-column PDF layout collapses cost glyphs). Total cost is best-effort, encoded as 'wild' (the rulebook's flexible-resource symbol) so each card parses against ResourceKind. Resource-type breakdown MUST be confirmed in the #17 OCR follow-up.
stats_confidence.keywords: MEDIUM — extracted from card effect text where named (Long-Range, Charge 2, Armor 2, Melee Armor 1). Watchman's 'Action: Replace a surrounding camp...' is recorded in flavor since unit cards do not carry effect payloads in the MVP-1 schema.

do_not: treat the cost numbers as canonical — encode as wild now, refine in #17
add behavior code (rules/cards/english/*.ts) — deferred per the assets-meta loader-shape decision (sabine-assets-meta-loader-shape.md)
ship real card art alongside this — placeholder strategy documented in apps/web/public/cards/english/README.md

needs_confirmation.note: cost icons ambiguous; STARTING SCOUT may imply free
Action: Replace camp -> barracks; modelled as flavor only

cards.name: Watchman
cards.flavor: STARTING SCOUT. Action: Replace a surrounding camp of yours with a barracks. — In 13th-century England, strict curfews gave rise to the Night Watch.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows multiple cost icons next to Watchman but values may belong to the adjacent Anglo-Normans civilization column. Watchman is labelled STARTING SCOUT which often implies free / pre-deployed in similar boardgames; cost encoded as wild:1 pending #17.
_needsConfirmation.effect.action: Watchman's 'Action: Replace a surrounding camp of yours with a barracks' is flavor-only in this MVP-1 data file. The unit schema does not carry effect payloads (only action/tactic/upgrade/event cards do). Confirm in #17 whether this should be modelled as a separate ability hook.

cards.name: Billman
cards.flavor: A bill was an agricultural tool used for trimming trees, later adapted into a weapon by adding spikes. While much of Europe adopted the pike, the English kept the bill as their primary polearm.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows three '1' cost icons (1+1+1). Encoded as wild:3 until #17 identifies which resources (wood/food/gold).

cards.name: Welsh Infantry
cards.flavor: Welsh infantry became a vital pillar of the English army. Primarily recruited as spearmen and foot-archers, thousands of these skilled warriors were drafted to fight the king's wars in Scotland and France.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows '2 1' cost icons (sum 3). Encoded as wild:3 until #17 resolves resource types.
_needsConfirmation.class.dual: Card band reads 'INFANTRY - ARCHER'. Modelled here as a class array ['infantry','archer']. Confirm in #17 whether the rules engine treats hybrid classes as union (any matching class effect applies) or intersection.

cards.name: Longbowman
cards.flavor: Longbows were a common weapon throughout history, most famously the yew longbows. Made from one piece of wood, they were quick and easy to craft.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows '2 2' cost icons (sum 4). Encoded as wild:4 until #17.

cards.name: Esquire
cards.flavor: Esquire was a title of respect for men of higher social rank. They were employed by nobles of higher rank, such as knights.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows '2 1' cost icons (sum 3). Encoded as wild:3 until #17.
_needsConfirmation.keywords.charge: Card text reads 'Charge 2 (+2 against units after moving 2 or more squares this turn)'. Encoded as 'charge-2' keyword; the rules engine will need a typed keyword vocabulary in a follow-up (see Wedge's card-effect-typing inbox note).

cards.name: English Knight
cards.flavor: Knighthood was considered a rank of minor nobility. A knight was often a vassal who served as an elite warrior or bodyguard to a lord in return for payment.

_needsConfirmation.cost.breakdown: OCR shows '3 4' cost icons (sum 7). Encoded as wild:7 until #17.
_needsConfirmation.keywords.armor: Card text reads 'Armor 2 (Take 2 less damage during combat)'. Modelled as 'armor-2' keyword string; structured armor handling deferred.

cards.name: Hobelar
cards.flavor: Light cavalry scouts mounted on hardy ponies, the hobelars ranged ahead of the main host to harass and report. Indispensable for the long marches into Scotland.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Stats picked to fit a light-cavalry scout role (mov 3, melee 2, health 3) distinct from the heavier English Knight; cost encoded as wild per existing rulebook-cost ambiguity convention.

cards.name: Pikeman
cards.flavor: A wall of bristling pikes could break a cavalry charge before it landed. Cheaper than knights and easier to muster from the shire levy.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. anti-cavalry keyword is placeholder vocabulary pending the typed keyword DSL.

cards.name: Yeoman
cards.flavor: Free landholders who trained at the village butts every Sunday by royal decree. Their longbows turned smallholdings into a strategic reserve.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Sits between Longbowman (specialist) and Welsh Infantry (hybrid) as a basic archer.

cards.name: Crossbowman
cards.flavor: Slower to reload than a longbow but punching clean through mail. Mercenary companies from Genoa filled out English ranks in the early wars.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. armor-piercing keyword is placeholder vocabulary; semantics TBD by the rules engine.

cards.name: Man-at-Arms
cards.flavor: Professional soldiers in full mail and plate, retained by lords as the backbone of any serious host.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. armor-1 keyword mirrors the English Knight's armor-2 convention.

cards.name: Mounted Sergeant
cards.flavor: Career soldiers under a knight's banner, mounted but lacking the title. Reliable shock cavalry without a knight's price tag.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Stat-line slots between Esquire and English Knight on the cavalry curve.

cards.name: Welsh Spearman
cards.flavor: Levied from the Welsh uplands, these spearmen formed bristling hedges that held the line while the longbows did their work.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Complements Welsh Infantry (hybrid) as a cheaper pure-infantry option.

cards.name: Border Reiver
cards.flavor: Light horsemen of the Anglo-Scottish marches, equally at home raiding cattle by moonlight or scouting for the king's army.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. raid keyword is placeholder vocabulary, semantics TBD.

cards.name: Foot Knight
cards.flavor: Dismounted men-at-arms in full harness, anchoring the centre of the line where horses could not reach.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. armor-2 mirrors English Knight; intentionally cheaper than the mounted version.

cards.name: Mounted Archer
cards.flavor: Horse and bow together — skirmishers who could harry a column for miles without ever offering a fight.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Hybrid cavalry/archer mirrors Welsh Infantry's hybrid pattern. skirmish keyword is placeholder vocabulary.

cards.name: Levy the Fyrd
cards.flavor: The king's writ summons every able-bodied freeman to his banner. Boots on the road within the week.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. The English card pool may not include an action card with this name; treat as schema-coverage filler until real data lands.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: 'Levy the Fyrd' → {draw, count:2} — direct mapping of the placeholder 'draw 2 cards' flavor to the locked Effect DSL.

cards.name: Shield Wall
cards.flavor: Locked shields, planted feet, the old Saxon discipline. The line holds or the line dies.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Deployment-only because the effect modifies a deployed unit; confirm phase against real card text.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: 'target infantry unit +1 health EoT' → buff-unit-stat over units-by-class(infantry, own). Per decisions.md, single-target unit selection defaults to units-by-class (set semantics). If/when a single-unit target with class filter is added to the DSL, this should narrow.

cards.name: Plate Armor
cards.flavor: Articulated steel covering shoulder to greave. Heavy, hot, and very hard to kill.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: attached unit gains armor-1 keyword. Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Class restriction mirrors the rulebook's class-restricted upgrade pattern; archers excluded as a thematic guess.

cards.name: Longbow Mastery
cards.flavor: Mandated weekly practice at the village butts. A generation of yeomen could draw a war bow at ninety pounds.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: all friendly archer units gain +1 ranged. Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-2 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. subType 'A' is the schema placeholder per TechnologySubType — real subtype taxonomy TBD.
